\"Error\"}\n63\t\tbytes, err := xml.Marshal(e)\n64\t\tif err != nil {\n65\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n66\t\t}\n67\t\treturn xml.Header + string(bytes), nil\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t// response.\n105\ttype LoggedResponse struct {\n106\t\tTimestamp   time.Time\n107\t\tStatusCode  int\n108\t\tHeaders     http.Header\n109\t\tBody        string\n110\t\tScenarioIdx int // Index of the scenario that generated this response\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// NewConfigurableErrorServer creates a new test server with configurable error responses.\n114\t//\n115\t// This helper function creates an HTTP test server that returns configured error responses\n116\t// based on the provided scenarios. It's useful for testing client error handling.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order, first match wins)\n120\t//\n121\t// Returns:\n122\t// &ConfigurableErrorServer{\n136\t\t\tScenarios:  scenarios,\n137\t\t\tRequestLog: make([]LoggedRequest, 0),\n138\t\t\tResponseLog: make([]LoggedResponse, 0),\n139\t\t}\n140\t\n141\t\thandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n142\t\t\ts.handleRequest(w, r)\n143\t\t})\n144\t\n145\t\ts.Server = httptest.NewServer(handler)\n146\t\treturn s\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t// NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault creates a server with a default scenario.\n150\t//\n151\t// This variant creates a server that uses a default scenario when no other scenarios match.\n152\t// Useful for testing with a primary error case and fallback behavior.\n153\t//\n154\t// Parameters:\n155\t//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order)\n156\t//   - defaultScenario: Fallback scenario when no other matches\n157\t//\n158\t// Returns:\n159\t//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n160\t//\n161\t// Example:\n162\t//\n163\t//\tdefaultScenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n164\t//\t    StatusCode: 500,\n165\t//\t    ErrorCode: \"InternalError\",\n166\t//\t    Message: \"Internal server error\",\n167\t//\t}\n168\t//\tserver := NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios, &defaultScenario)\n169\t//\tdefer server.Close()\n170\tfunc NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario, defaultScenario *ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n171\t\ts := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n172\t\ts.DefaultScenario = defaultScenario\n173\t\treturn s\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// handleRequest processes an incoming request and responds with the appropriate error.\n177\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) handleRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n178\t\ts.RequestCount++\n179\t\n180\t\t// Log the request\n181\t\ts.logRequest(r)\n182\t\n183\t\t// Find matching scenario\n184\t\tscenario, scenarioIdx := s.findMatchingScenario(r)\n185\t\n186\t\t// Apply delay if configured\n187\t\tif scenario.Delay > 0 {\n188\t\t\ttime.Sleep(scenario.Delay)\n189\t\t}\n190\t\n191\t\t// Set custom headers FIRST (before WriteHeader)\n192\t\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n193\t\t\tw.Header().Set(key, value)\n194\t\t}\n195\t\n196\t\t// Set default Content-Type if not set\n197\t\tif w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\") == \"\" {\n198\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n199\t\t}\n200\t\n201\t\t// Set status code LAST (after headers)\n202\t\tw.WriteHeader(scenario.StatusCode)\n203\t\n204\t\t// Write response body\n205\t\tbody := s.getResponseBody(scenario)\n206\t\tw.Write([]byte(body))\n207\t\n208\t\t// Log the response\n209\t\ts.logResponse(scenario, scenarioIdx, body)\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// findMatchingScenario finds the first scenario that matches the request.\n213\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) findMatchingScenario(r *http.Request) (ErrorServerScenario, int) {\n214\t\tfor i, scenario := range s.Scenarios {\n215\t\t\tif scenario.RequestMatcher == nil || scenario.RequestMatcher(r) {\n216\t\t\t\treturn scenario, i\n217\t\t\t}\n218\t\t}\n219\t\n220\t\t// Use default if no match\n221\t\tif s.DefaultScenario != nil {\n222\t\t\treturn *s.DefaultScenario, -1\n223\t\t}\n224\t\n225\t\t// Fallback to 500 error\n226\t\treturn ErrorServerScenario{\n227\t\t\tStatusCode: 500,\n228\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"InternalError\",\n229\t\t\tMessage:    \"No matching scenario found\",\n230\t\t}, -1\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// getResponseBody generates the response body for a scenario.\n234\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) getResponseBody(scenario ErrorServerScenario) string {\n235\t\t// Use custom body if provided\n236\t\tif scenario.BodyOverride != \"\" {\n237\t\t\treturn scenario.BodyOverride\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Generate S3 error XML\n241\t\ts3Err := S3ErrorResponse{\n242\t\t\tCode:     scenario.ErrorCode,\n243\t\t\tMessage:  scenario.Message,\n244\t\t\tResource: scenario.Resource,\n245\t\t\tRequestId: fmt.Sprintf(\"req-%d\", s.RequestCount),\n246\t\t}\n247\t\n248\t\t// Add additional fields from headers if they exist\n249\t\tif method, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Method\"]; ok {\n250\t\t\ts3Err.Method = method\n251\t\t}\n252\t\tif allowedMethods, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Allowed-Methods\"]; ok {\n253\t\t\ts3Err.AllowedMethods = allowedMethods\n254\t\t}\n255\t\tif contentType, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Content-Type\"]; ok {\n256\t\t\ts3Err.ContentType = contentType\n257\t\t}\n258\t\n259\t\txml, err := ToXMLWithError(s3Err)\n260\t\tif err != nil {\n261\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\"?><Error><Code>InternalError</Code><Message>Failed to generate error response: %v</Message></Error>\", err)\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\treturn xml\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t// logRequest logs an incoming request.\n268\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logRequest(r *http.Request) {\n269\t\tbodyBytes, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n270\t\tr.Body.Close()\n271\t\n272\t\tloggedReq := LoggedRequest{\n273\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n274\t\t\tMethod:    r.Method,\n275\t\t\tPath:      r.URL.Path,\n276\t\t\tHeaders:   r.Header.Clone(),\n277\t\t\tBody:      string(bodyBytes),\n278\t\t}\n279\t\n280\t\ts.RequestLog = append(s.RequestLog, loggedReq)\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// logResponse logs an outgoing response.\n284\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logResponse(scenario ErrorServerScenario, scenarioIdx int, body string) {\n285\t\tloggedResp := LoggedResponse{\n286\t\t\tTimestamp:   time.Now(),\n287\t\t\tStatusCode:  scenario.StatusCode,\n288\t\t\tHeaders:     make(http.Header),\n289\t\t\tBody:        body,\n290\t\t\tScenarioIdx: scenarioIdx,\n291\t\t}\n292\t\n293\t\t// Copy headers\n294\t\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n295\t\t\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(key, value)\n296\t\t}\n297\t\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n298\t\n299\t\ts.ResponseLog = append(s.ResponseLog, loggedResp)\n300\t}\n301\t\n302\t// Close closes the test server.\n303\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Close() {\n304\t\ts.Server.Close()\n305\t}\n306\t\n307\t// URL returns the URL of the test server.\n308\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) URL() string {\n309\t\treturn s.Server.URL\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\t// Reset resets the request count and logs.\n313\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Reset() {\n314\t\ts.RequestCount = 0\n315\t\ts.RequestLog = make([]LoggedRequest, 0)\n316\t\ts.ResponseLog = make([]LoggedResponse, 0)\n317\t}\n318\t\n319\t// GetLastRequest returns the most recent request log.\n320\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastRequest() *LoggedRequest {\n321\t\tif len(s.RequestLog) == 0 {\n322\t\t\treturn nil\n323\t\t}\n324\t\treturn &s.RequestLog[len(s.RequestLog)-1]\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// GetLastResponse returns the most recent response log.\n328\tfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastResponse() *LoggedResponse {\n329\t\tif len(s.ResponseLog) == 0 {\n330\t\t\treturn nil\n331\t\t}\n332\t\treturn &s.ResponseLog[len(s.ResponseLog)-1]\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t// PREDEFINED ERROR SCENARIOS\n337\t// =============================================================================\n338\t\n339\t// PredefinedErrorScenarios provides common error scenarios for testing.\n340\tvar PredefinedErrorScenarios = struct {\n341\t\t// BadRequest represents a 400 Bad Request error\n342\t\tBadRequest ErrorServerScenario\n343\t\n344\t\t// Unauthorized represents a 401 Unauthorized error\n345\t\tUnauthorized ErrorServerScenario\n346\t\n347\t\t// Forbidden represents a 403 Forbidden error\n348\t\tForbidden ErrorServerScenario\n349\t\n350\t\t// NotFound represents a 404 Not Found error\n351\t\tNotFound ErrorServerScenario\n352\t\n353\t\t// InternalServerError represents a 500 Internal Server Error\n354\t\tInternalServerError ErrorServerScenario\n355\t\n356\t\t// MethodNotAllowed represents a 405 Method Not Allowed error\n357\t\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorServerScenario\n358\t\n359\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType represents a 415 Unsupported Media Type error\n360\t\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorServerScenario\n361\t\n362\t\t// ServiceUnavailable represents a 503 Service Unavailable error\n363\t\tServiceUnavailable ErrorServerScenario\n364\t}{\n365\t\t// BadRequest: 400 Bad Request\n366\t\tBadRequest: ErrorServerScenario{\n367\t\t\tStatusCode: 400,\n368\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"InvalidRequest\",\n369\t\t\tMessage:    \"The request is invalid or malformed\",\n370\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n371\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n372\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n373\t\t\t},\n374\t\t},\n375\t\n376\t\t// Unauthorized: 401 Unauthorized\n377\t\tUnauthorized: ErrorServerScenario{\n378\t\t\tStatusCode: 401,\n379\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"Unauthorized\",\n380\t\t\tMessage:    \"Authentication is required for this request\",\n381\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n382\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n383\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n384\t\t\t\t\"WWW-Authenticate\": \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n385\t\t\t},\n386\t\t},\n387\t\n388\t\t// Forbidden: 403 Forbidden\n389\t\tForbidden: ErrorServerScenario{\n390\t\t\tStatusCode: 403,\n391\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"AccessDenied\",\n392\t\t\tMessage:    \"Access Denied - You do not have permission to access this resource\",\n393\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n394\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n395\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n396\t\t\t},\n397\t\t},\n398\t\n399\t\t// NotFound: 404 Not Found\n400\t\tNotFound: ErrorServerScenario{\n401\t\t\tStatusCode: 404,\n402\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"NoSuchKey\",\n403\t\t\tMessage:    \"The specified key does not exist\",\n404\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n405\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n406\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n407\t\t\t},\n408\t\t},\n409\t\n410\t\t// InternalServerError: 500 Internal Server Error\n411\t\tInternalServerError: ErrorServerScenario{\n412\t\t\tStatusCode: 500,\n413\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"InternalError\",\n414\t\t\tMessage:    \"An internal server error occurred. Please try again later.\",\n415\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n416\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n417\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n418\t\t\t},\n419\t\t},\n420\t\n421\t\t// MethodNotAllowed: 405 Method Not Allowed\n422\t\tMethodNotAllowed: ErrorServerScenario{\n423\t\t\tStatusCode: 405,\n424\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n425\t\t\tMessage:    \"The specified method is not allowed against this resource\",\n426\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n427\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n428\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n429\t\t\t\t\"Allow\": \"GET, HEAD, PUT, DELETE\",\n430\t\t\t\t\"X-Error-Allowed-Methods\": \"GET, HEAD, PUT, DELETE\",\n431\t\t\t\t\"X-Error-Method\": \"POST\",\n432\t\t\t},\n433\t\t},\n434\t\n435\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType: 415 Unsupported Media Type\n436\t\tUnsupportedMediaType: ErrorServerScenario{\n437\t\t\tStatusCode: 415,\n438\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"UnsupportedMediaType\",\n439\t\t\tMessage:    \"The content type is not supported\",\n440\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n441\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n442\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n443\t\t\t\t\"X-Error-Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n444\t\t\t},\n445\t\t},\n446\t\n447\t\t// ServiceUnavailable: 503 Service Unavailable\n448\t\tServiceUnavailable: ErrorServerScenario{\n449\t\t\tStatusCode: 503,\n450\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"ServiceUnavailable\",\n451\t\t\tMessage:    \"The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.\",\n452\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n453\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n454\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n455\t\t\t\t\"Retry-After\": \"60\",\n456\t\t\t},\n457\t\t},\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// =============================================================================\n461\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t\n464\t// NewSingleErrorServer creates a server that always returns the same error.\n465\t//\n466\t// This is a convenience function for creating a simple test server with a single\n467\t// error response. Useful for basic error handling tests.\n468\t//\n469\t// Parameters:\n470\t//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to return\n471\t//   - errorCode: S3 error code\n472\t//   - message: Error message\n473\t//\n474\t// Returns:\n475\t//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n476\t//\n477\t// Example:\n478\t//\n479\t//\tserver := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Resource not found\")\n480\t//\tdefer server.Close()\n481\t//\n482\t//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n483\tfunc NewSingleErrorServer(statusCode int, errorCode string, message string) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n484\t\tscenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n485\t\t\tStatusCode: statusCode,\n486\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n487\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n488\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n489\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n490\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n491\t\t\t},\n492\t\t}\n493\t\n494\t\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer([]ErrorServerScenario{scenario})\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t// NewMultipleErrorServer creates a server with multiple error scenarios.\n498\t//\n499\t// This convenience function creates a server that responds with different errors\n500\t// based on request matching. Each scenario is evaluated in order.\n501\t//\n502\t// Parameters:\n503\t//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios with request matchers\n504\t//\n505\t// Returns:\n506\t//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n507\t//\n508\t// Example:\n509\t//\n510\t//\tscenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n511\t//\t    {\n512\t//\t        StatusCode: 404,\n513\t//\t        ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n514\t//\t        Message: \"Not found\",\n515\t//\t        RequestMatcher: func(r *http.Request) bool {\n516\t//\t            return strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, \"/missing\")\n517\t//\t        },\n518\t//\t    },\n519\t//\t    {\n520\t//\t        StatusCode: 403,\n521\t//\t        ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\",\n522\t//\t        Message: \"Access denied\",\n523\t//\t        RequestMatcher: func(r *http.Request) bool {\n524\t//\t            return strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, \"/forbidden\")\n525\t//\t        },\n526\t//\t    },\n527\t//\t}\n528\t//\tserver := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n529\t//\tdefer server.Close()\n530\tfunc NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n531\t\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t// NewDelayErrorServer creates a server that adds delay to responses.\n535\t//\n536\t// This is useful for testing timeout handling and slow response scenarios.\n537\t//\n538\t// Parameters:\n539\t//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to return\n540\t//   - errorCode: S3 error code\n541\t//   - message: Error message\n542\t//   - delay: Delay before responding\n543\t//\n544\t// Returns:\n545\t//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n546\t//\n547\t// Example:\n548\t//\n549\t//\tserver := NewDelayErrorServer(500, \"InternalError\", \"Server error\", 2*time.Second)\n550\t//\tdefer server.Close()\n551\t//\n552\t//\tclient := &http.Client{Timeout: 1 * time.Second}\n553\t//\tresp, err := client.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\") // Will timeout\n554\tfunc NewDelayErrorServer(statusCode int, errorCode string, message string, delay time.Duration) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n555\t\tscenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n556\t\t\tStatusCode: statusCode,\n557\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n558\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n559\t\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n560\t\t\tDelay:      delay,\n561\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n562\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n563\t\t\t},\n564\t\t}\n565\t\n566\t\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer([]ErrorServerScenario{scenario})\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t// =============================================================================\n570\t// REQUEST MATCHERS\n571\t// =============================================================================\n572\t\n573\t// MatchByPath creates a request matcher that matches by URL path.\n574\tfunc MatchByPath(path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n575\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n576\t\t\treturn r.URL.Path == path\n577\t\t}\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// MatchByPathPrefix creates a request matcher that matches by path prefix.\n581\tfunc MatchByPathPrefix(prefix string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n582\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n583\t\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(r.URL.Path, prefix)\n584\t\t}\n585\t}\n586\t\n587\t// MatchByMethod creates a request matcher that matches by HTTP method.\n588\tfunc MatchByMethod(method string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n589\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n590\t\t\treturn r.Method == method\n591\t\t}\n592\t}\n593\t\n594\t// MatchByMethodAndPath creates a request matcher that matches both method and path.\n595\tfunc MatchByMethodAndPath(method, path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n596\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n597\t\t\treturn r.Method == method && r.URL.Path == path\n598\t\t}\n599\t}\n600\t\n601\t// MatchByHeader creates a request matcher that matches by header value.\n602\tfunc MatchByHeader(header, value string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n603\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n604\t\t\treturn r.Header.Get(header) == value\n605\t\t}\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t// MatchAlways creates a request matcher that always matches.\n609\tfunc MatchAlways() func(*http.Request) bool {\n610\t\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n611\t\t\treturn true\n612\t\t}\n613\t}\n614\t\n615\t// =============================================================================\n616\t// VALIDATION HELPERS\n617\t// =============================================================================\n618\t\n619\t// ValidateErrorServerResponse validates a response from the error server.\n620\t//\n621\t// This helper validates that a response from the error server matches expectations.\n622\t// It checks status code, content type, and S3 error structure.\n623\t//\n624\t// Parameters:\n625\t//   - t: Testing instance\n626\t//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n627\t//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code\n628\t//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code\n629\t//\n630\t// Example:\n631\t//\n632\t//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n633\t//\tif err != nil {\n634\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n635\t//\t}\n636\t//\tValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n637\tfunc ValidateErrorServerResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedErrorCode string) {\n638\t\tt.Helper()\n639\t\n640\t\t// Validate status code\n641\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n642\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n643\t\t}\n644\t\n645\t\t// Validate content type\n646\t\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n647\t\tif contentType != \"application/xml\" {\n648\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Content-Type 'application/xml', got '%s'\", contentType)\n649\t\t}\n650\t\n651\t\t// Parse and validate S3 error\n652\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n653\t\tif err != nil {\n654\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n655\t\t}\n656\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n657\t\n658\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n659\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n660\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error response: %v\", err)\n661\t\t}\n662\t\n663\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedErrorCode {\n664\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedErrorCode, s3Err.Code)\n665\t\t}\n666\t\n667\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n668\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected non-empty error message\")\n669\t\t}\n670\t}\n671\t\n672\t// GetErrorServerS3Error extracts and returns the S3 error from a response.\n673\t//\n674\t// This helper reads and parses the S3 error from an HTTP response.\n675\t// Useful for assertions in tests.\n676\t//\n677\t// Parameters:\n678\t//   - t: Testing instance\n679\t//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error from\n680\t//\n681\t// Returns:\n682\t//   - *S3Error: Parsed S3 error structure\n683\t//\n684\t// Example:\n685\t//\n686\t//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n687\t//\tif err != nil {\n688\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n689\t//\t}\n690\t//\ts3Err := GetErrorServerS3Error(t, resp)\n691\t//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n692\t//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n693\t//\t}\n694\tfunc GetErrorServerS3Error(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) *S3Error {\n695\t\tt.Helper()\n696\t\n697\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n698\t\tif err != nil {\n699\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n700\t\t}\n701\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n702\t\n703\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n704\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n705\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error response: %v\", err)\n706\t\t}\n707\t\n708\t\treturn &s3Err\n709\t}\n710\t\n711\t// =============================================================================\n712\t// DOCUMENTATION\n713\t// =============================================================================\n714\t\n715\t/*\n716\tUsage Examples:\n717\t\n718\t1. Simple single error server:\n719\t\n720\t    server := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Resource not found\")\n721\t    defer server.Close()\n722\t\n723\t    resp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n724\t    if err != nil {\n725\t        t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n726\t    }\n727\t    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n728\t\n729\t2. Using predefined scenarios:\n768\t\n769\t    scenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n770\t        PredefinedErrorScenarios.NotFound,\n771\t        PredefinedErrorScenarios.Forbidden,\n772\t    }\n773\t    server := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n774\t    defer server.Close()\n775\t\n776\t5. Checking request logs:\n777\t\n778\t    server := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Not found\")\n779\t    defer server.Close()\n780\t\n781\t    http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n782\t\n783\t    lastReq := server.GetLastRequest()\n784\t    if lastReq.Path != \"/resource\" {\n785\t        t.Errorf(\"Expected path /resource, got %s\", lastReq.Path)\n786\t    }\n787\t\n788\t6. ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n800\t            Message: \"Not found\",\n801\t            RequestMatcher: MatchByMethodAndPath(\"GET\", \"/resource\"),\n802\t        },\n803\t    }\n804\t    server := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n805\t    defer server.Close()\n806\t\n807\t    // POST returns 405\n808\t    resp, _ := http.Post(server.URL+\"/resource\", \"application/xml\", nil)\n809\t    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 405, \"MethodNotAllowed\")\n810\t\n811\t    // GET returns 404\n812\t    resp, _ = http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n813\t    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n814\t\n815\tAvailable Predefined Scenarios:\n816\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.BadRequest (400)\n817\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.Unauthorized (401)\n818\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.Forbidden (403)\n819\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.NotFound (404)\n820\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.InternalServerError (500)\n821\t- PredefinedErrorScenarios.MethodNotAllowed server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SERVER SETUP HELPER\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides helpers for setting up test servers with configurable\n// error responses. These helpers are useful for testing client code that\n// needs to handle various HTTP error scenarios.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   1. Use NewConfigurableErrorServer() to create a server with custom error responses\n//   2. Use predefined error scenarios for common cases\n//   3. Configure custom error scenarios as needed\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO CONFIGURATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorServerScenario defines the configuration for an error response scenario.\ntype ErrorServerScenario struct {\n\t// StatusCode is the HTTP status code to return\n\tStatusCode int\n\n\t// ErrorCode is the S3 error code to include in the response\n\tErrorCode string\n\n\t// Message is the error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Resource is the resource path that caused the error (optional)\n\tResource string\n\n\t// Headers are additional HTTP headers to include (optional)\n\tHeaders map[string]string\n\n\t// Delay is the artificial delay before responding (useful for testing timeouts)\n\tDelay time.Duration\n\n\t// BodyOverride is a custom response body (optional, overrides automatic XML generation)\n\tBodyOverride string\n\n\t// RequestMatcher is a function to match incoming requests (optional)\n\t// If nil, all requests will match this scenario\n\tRequestMatcher func(*http.Request) bool\n}\n\n// ToXMLWithError converts the S3 error response to XML format with error handling.\n// This is a helper function for generating S3 XML responses.\nfunc ToXMLWithError(e S3ErrorResponse) (string, error) {\n\te.XMLName = xml.Name{Local: \"Error\"}\n\tbytes, err := xml.Marshal(e)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\treturn xml.Header + string(bytes), nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SERVER SETUP\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ConfigurableErrorServer is a test server that returns configured error responses.\ntype ConfigurableErrorServer struct {\n\t// Server is the underlying httptest server\n\tServer *httptest.Server\n\n\t// Scenarios is the list of error scenarios in priority order\n\tScenarios []ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// DefaultScenario is used when no other scenario matches\n\tDefaultScenario *ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// RequestCount tracks the number of requests handled\n\tRequestCount int\n\n\t// RequestLog contains a log of all requests received\n\tRequestLog []LoggedRequest\n\n\t// ResponseLog contains a log of all responses sent\n\tResponseLog []LoggedResponse\n}\n\n// LoggedRequest represents a logged incoming request.\ntype LoggedRequest struct {\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n\tMethod    string\n\tPath      string\n\tHeaders   http.Header\n\tBody      string\n}\n\n// LoggedResponse represents a logged outgoing response.\ntype LoggedResponse struct {\n\tTimestamp   time.Time\n\tStatusCode  int\n\tHeaders     http.Header\n\tBody        string\n\tScenarioIdx int // Index of the scenario that generated this response\n}\n\n// NewConfigurableErrorServer creates a new test server with configurable error responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates an HTTP test server that returns configured error responses\n// based on the provided scenarios. It's useful for testing client error handling.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order, first match wins)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tscenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n//\t    {StatusCode: 404, ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\", Message: \"Not found\"},\n//\t    {StatusCode: 403, ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\", Message: \"Access denied\"},\n//\t}\n//\tserver := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\n//\n//\t// Make requests to server.URL\nfunc NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\ts := &ConfigurableErrorServer{\n\t\tScenarios:  scenarios,\n\t\tRequestLog: make([]LoggedRequest, 0),\n\t\tResponseLog: make([]LoggedResponse, 0),\n\t}\n\n\thandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\ts.handleRequest(w, r)\n\t})\n\n\ts.Server = httptest.NewServer(handler)\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault creates a server with a default scenario.\n//\n// This variant creates a server that uses a default scenario when no other scenarios match.\n// Useful for testing with a primary error case and fallback behavior.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order)\n//   - defaultScenario: Fallback scenario when no other matches\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tdefaultScenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n//\t    StatusCode: 500,\n//\t    ErrorCode: \"InternalError\",\n//\t    Message: \"Internal server error\",\n//\t}\n//\tserver := NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios, &defaultScenario)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\nfunc NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario, defaultScenario *ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\ts := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n\ts.DefaultScenario = defaultScenario\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// handleRequest processes an incoming request and responds with the appropriate error.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) handleRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\ts.RequestCount++\n\n\t// Log the request\n\ts.logRequest(r)\n\n\t// Find matching scenario\n\tscenario, scenarioIdx := s.findMatchingScenario(r)\n\n\t// Apply delay if configured\n\tif scenario.Delay > 0 {\n\t\ttime.Sleep(scenario.Delay)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers FIRST (before WriteHeader)\n\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set default Content-Type if not set\n\tif w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\") == \"\" {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Set status code LAST (after headers)\n\tw.WriteHeader(scenario.StatusCode)\n\n\t// Write response body\n\tbody := s.getResponseBody(scenario)\n\tw.Write([]byte(body))\n\n\t// Log the response\n\ts.logResponse(scenario, scenarioIdx, body)\n}\n\n// findMatchingScenario finds the first scenario that matches the request.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) findMatchingScenario(r *http.Request) (ErrorServerScenario, int) {\n\tfor i, scenario := range s.Scenarios {\n\t\tif scenario.RequestMatcher == nil || scenario.RequestMatcher(r) {\n\t\t\treturn scenario, i\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use default if no match\n\tif s.DefaultScenario != nil {\n\t\treturn *s.DefaultScenario, -1\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback to 500 error\n\treturn ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 500,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"InternalError\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"No matching scenario found\",\n\t}, -1\n}\n\n// getResponseBody generates the response body for a scenario.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) getResponseBody(scenario ErrorServerScenario) string {\n\t// Use custom body if provided\n\tif scenario.BodyOverride != \"\" {\n\t\treturn scenario.BodyOverride\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate S3 error XML\n\ts3Err := S3ErrorResponse{\n\t\tCode:     scenario.ErrorCode,\n\t\tMessage:  scenario.Message,\n\t\tResource: scenario.Resource,\n\t\tRequestId: fmt.Sprintf(\"req-%d\", s.RequestCount),\n\t}\n\n\t// Add additional fields from headers if they exist\n\tif method, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Method\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.Method = method\n\t}\n\tif allowedMethods, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Allowed-Methods\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.AllowedMethods = allowedMethods\n\t}\n\tif contentType, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Content-Type\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.ContentType = contentType\n\t}\n\n\txml, err := ToXMLWithError(s3Err)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\"?><Error><Code>InternalError</Code><Message>Failed to generate error response: %v</Message></Error>\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn xml\n}\n\n// logRequest logs an incoming request.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logRequest(r *http.Request) {\n\tbodyBytes, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tr.Body.Close()\n\n\tloggedReq := LoggedRequest{\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\tMethod:    r.Method,\n\t\tPath:      r.URL.Path,\n\t\tHeaders:   r.Header.Clone(),\n\t\tBody:      string(bodyBytes),\n\t}\n\n\ts.RequestLog = append(s.RequestLog, loggedReq)\n}\n\n// logResponse logs an outgoing response.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logResponse(scenario ErrorServerScenario, scenarioIdx int, body string) {\n\tloggedResp := LoggedResponse{\n\t\tTimestamp:   time.Now(),\n\t\tStatusCode:  scenario.StatusCode,\n\t\tHeaders:     make(http.Header),\n\t\tBody:        body,\n\t\tScenarioIdx: scenarioIdx,\n\t}\n\n\t// Copy headers\n\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n\t\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\n\ts.ResponseLog = append(s.ResponseLog, loggedResp)\n}\n\n// Close closes the test server.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Close() {\n\ts.Server.Close()\n}\n\n// URL returns the URL of the test server.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) URL() string {\n\treturn s.Server.URL\n}\n\n// Reset resets the request count and logs.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Reset() {\n\ts.RequestCount = 0\n\ts.RequestLog = make([]LoggedRequest, 0)\n\ts.ResponseLog = make([]LoggedResponse, 0)\n}\n\n// GetLastRequest returns the most recent request log.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastRequest() *LoggedRequest {\n\tif len(s.RequestLog) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &s.RequestLog[len(s.RequestLog)-1]\n}\n\n// GetLastResponse returns the most recent response log.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastResponse() *LoggedResponse {\n\tif len(s.ResponseLog) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &s.ResponseLog[len(s.ResponseLog)-1]\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// PREDEFINED ERROR SCENARIOS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// PredefinedErrorScenarios provides common error scenarios for testing.\nvar PredefinedErrorScenarios = struct {\n\t// BadRequest represents a 400 Bad Request error\n\tBadRequest ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// Unauthorized represents a 401 Unauthorized error\n\tUnauthorized ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// Forbidden represents a 403 Forbidden error\n\tForbidden ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// NotFound represents a 404 Not Found error\n\tNotFound ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// InternalServerError represents a 500 Internal Server Error\n\tInternalServerError ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed represents a 405 Method Not Allowed error\n\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType represents a 415 Unsupported Media Type error\n\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// ServiceUnavailable represents a 503 Service Unavailable error\n\tServiceUnavailable ErrorServerScenario\n}{\n\t// BadRequest: 400 Bad Request\n\tBadRequest: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 400,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"InvalidRequest\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"The request is invalid or malformed\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// Unauthorized: 401 Unauthorized\n\tUnauthorized: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 401,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"Unauthorized\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"Authentication is required for this request\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t\t\"WWW-Authenticate\": \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// Forbidden: 403 Forbidden\n\tForbidden: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 403,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"AccessDenied\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"Access Denied - You do not have permission to access this resource\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// NotFound: 404 Not Found\n\tNotFound: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 404,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"NoSuchKey\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"The specified key does not exist\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// InternalServerError: 500 Internal Server Error\n\tInternalServerError: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 500,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"InternalError\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"An internal server error occurred. Please try again later.\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed: 405 Method Not Allowed\n\tMethodNotAllowed: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 405,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"The specified method is not allowed against this resource\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t\t\"Allow\": \"GET, HEAD, PUT, DELETE\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Error-Allowed-Methods\": \"GET, HEAD, PUT, DELETE\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Error-Method\": \"POST\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType: 415 Unsupported Media Type\n\tUnsupportedMediaType: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 415,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"UnsupportedMediaType\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"The content type is not supported\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Error-Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n\n\t// ServiceUnavailable: 503 Service Unavailable\n\tServiceUnavailable: ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 503,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"ServiceUnavailable\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.\",\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t\t\"Retry-After\": \"60\",\n\t\t},\n\t},\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// NewSingleErrorServer creates a server that always returns the same error.\n//\n// This is a convenience function for creating a simple test server with a single\n// error response. Useful for basic error handling tests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to return\n//   - errorCode: S3 error code\n//   - message: Error message\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tserver := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Resource not found\")\n//\tdefer server.Close()\n//\n//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\nfunc NewSingleErrorServer(statusCode int, errorCode string, message string) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\tscenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: statusCode,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer([]ErrorServerScenario{scenario})\n}\n\n// NewMultipleErrorServer creates a server with multiple error scenarios.\n//\n// This convenience function creates a server that responds with different errors\n// based on request matching. Each scenario is evaluated in order.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios with request matchers\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tscenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n//\t    {\n//\t        StatusCode: 404,\n//\t        ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n//\t        Message: \"Not found\",\n//\t        RequestMatcher: func(r *http.Request) bool {\n//\t            return strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, \"/missing\")\n//\t        },\n//\t    },\n//\t    {\n//\t        StatusCode: 403,\n//\t        ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\",\n//\t        Message: \"Access denied\",\n//\t        RequestMatcher: func(r *http.Request) bool {\n//\t            return strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, \"/forbidden\")\n//\t        },\n//\t    },\n//\t}\n//\tserver := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\nfunc NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n}\n\n// NewDelayErrorServer creates a server that adds delay to responses.\n//\n// This is useful for testing timeout handling and slow response scenarios.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to return\n//   - errorCode: S3 error code\n//   - message: Error message\n//   - delay: Delay before responding\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tserver := NewDelayErrorServer(500, \"InternalError\", \"Server error\", 2*time.Second)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\n//\n//\tclient := &http.Client{Timeout: 1 * time.Second}\n//\tresp, err := client.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\") // Will timeout\nfunc NewDelayErrorServer(statusCode int, errorCode string, message string, delay time.Duration) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\tscenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: statusCode,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tResource:   \"/api/resource\",\n\t\tDelay:      delay,\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/xml\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\treturn NewConfigurableErrorServer([]ErrorServerScenario{scenario})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST MATCHERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// MatchByPath creates a request matcher that matches by URL path.\nfunc MatchByPath(path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.URL.Path == path\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByPathPrefix creates a request matcher that matches by path prefix.\nfunc MatchByPathPrefix(prefix string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(r.URL.Path, prefix)\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByMethod creates a request matcher that matches by HTTP method.\nfunc MatchByMethod(method string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Method == method\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByMethodAndPath creates a request matcher that matches both method and path.\nfunc MatchByMethodAndPath(method, path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Method == method && r.URL.Path == path\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByHeader creates a request matcher that matches by header value.\nfunc MatchByHeader(header, value string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Header.Get(header) == value\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchAlways creates a request matcher that always matches.\nfunc MatchAlways() func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateErrorServerResponse validates a response from the error server.\n//\n// This helper validates that a response from the error server matches expectations.\n// It checks status code, content type, and S3 error structure.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code\n//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateErrorServerResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedErrorCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType != \"application/xml\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Content-Type 'application/xml', got '%s'\", contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and validate S3 error\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedErrorCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedErrorCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected non-empty error message\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetErrorServerS3Error extracts and returns the S3 error from a response.\n//\n// This helper reads and parses the S3 error from an HTTP response.\n// Useful for assertions in tests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *S3Error: Parsed S3 error structure\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\ts3Err := GetErrorServerS3Error(t, resp)\n//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n//\t}\nfunc GetErrorServerS3Error(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\nUsage Examples:\n\n1. Simple single error server:\n\n    server := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Resource not found\")\n    defer server.Close()\n\n    resp, err := http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n    if err != nil {\n        t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n    }\n    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n\n2. Multiple error scenarios with path matching:\n\n    scenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n        {\n            StatusCode: 404,\n            ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n            Message: \"Not found\",\n            RequestMatcher: MatchByPath(\"/missing\"),\n        },\n        {\n            StatusCode: 403,\n            ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\",\n            Message: \"Access denied\",\n            RequestMatcher: MatchByPath(\"/forbidden\"),\n        },\n    }\n    server := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n    defer server.Close()\n\n    // Test 404 scenario\n    resp, _ := http.Get(server.URL + \"/missing\")\n    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n\n    // Test 403 scenario\n    resp, _ = http.Get(server.URL + \"/forbidden\")\n    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 403, \"AccessDenied\")\n\n3. Server with delay for timeout testing:\n\n    server := NewDelayErrorServer(500, \"InternalError\", \"Server error\", 2*time.Second)\n    defer server.Close()\n\n    client := &http.Client{Timeout: 1 * time.Second}\n    resp, err := client.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n    if err == nil {\n        t.Error(\"Expected timeout error\")\n    }\n\n4. Using predefined scenarios:\n\n    scenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n        PredefinedErrorScenarios.NotFound,\n        PredefinedErrorScenarios.Forbidden,\n    }\n    server := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n    defer server.Close()\n\n5. Checking request logs:\n\n    server := NewSingleErrorServer(404, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Not found\")\n    defer server.Close()\n\n    http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n\n    lastReq := server.GetLastRequest()\n    if lastReq.Path != \"/resource\" {\n        t.Errorf(\"Expected path /resource, got %s\", lastReq.Path)\n    }\n\n6. Method and path matching:\n\n    scenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n        {\n            StatusCode: 405,\n            ErrorCode: \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n            Message: \"Method not allowed\",\n            RequestMatcher: MatchByMethodAndPath(\"POST\", \"/resource\"),\n        },\n        {\n            StatusCode: 404,\n            ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n            Message: \"Not found\",\n            RequestMatcher: MatchByMethodAndPath(\"GET\", \"/resource\"),\n        },\n    }\n    server := NewMultipleErrorServer(scenarios)\n    defer server.Close()\n\n    // POST returns 405\n    resp, _ := http.Post(server.URL+\"/resource\", \"application/xml\", nil)\n    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 405, \"MethodNotAllowed\")\n\n    // GET returns 404\n    resp, _ = http.Get(server.URL + \"/resource\")\n    ValidateErrorServerResponse(t, resp, 404, \"NoSuchKey\")\n\nAvailable Predefined Scenarios:\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.BadRequest (400)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.Unauthorized (401)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.Forbidden (403)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.NotFound (404)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.InternalServerError (500)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.MethodNotAllowed (405)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.UnsupportedMediaType (415)\n- PredefinedErrorScenarios.ServiceUnavailable (503)\n*/\n","numLines":825,"startLine":1,"totalLines":825}}}

Use predefined patterns when possible for consistency\n77\t// 2. Extend patterns with custom configurations rather than creating from scratch\n78\t// 3. Use category-based pattern access for organized testing\n79\t// 4. Leverage table-driven tests for comprehensive coverage\n80\t// 5.
